If you find the worktree's tests render without these vars resolving, add fallback color literals matching the LCM color tokens already in `shared-styles.ts`.

# Verification before commit

```bash
npm test
npm run build
prek run --files ts/slot-card.ts ts/slot-card.styles.ts ts/slot-card.test.ts custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js
```

The `npm run build` regenerates the bundle at `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` — stage that file in the same commit (the project tracks the built output).

# Commit

```bash
git add ts/slot-card.ts ts/slot-card.styles.ts ts/slot-card.test.ts custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/
git commit -m "feat(card): redesign header with slot kicker, state chip, and primary name"
```

# Reporting back

Status: `DONE` / `DONE_WITH_CONCERNS` / `NEEDS_CONTEXT` / `BLOCKED`. Include:
- Vitest test count before vs after (any tests deleted? any new tests added?)
- Build success
- prek result
- Commit SHA
- Any concerns about CSS variable fallbacks, deleted tests, or design decisions you made that the plan didn't explicitly cover

If you have questions before proceeding, ask them and stop — do not implement guesses.

content.text:
---

**Status: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS**

**Vitest:**
- Baseline: 565 tests pass
- After: 570 tests pass
- Tests deleted: 2 (the `_navigateToEventHistory` describe block in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` — that helper was the only caller of the now-deleted clock pill `@click=${() => lastUsed && this._navigateToEventHistory()}` handler in the old header; Task 6 will reintroduce that navigation in the new Activity event row)
- Tests added: 7 (in `slot-card.integration.test.ts`, new `header redesign (slot kicker + state chip + name)` describe block — kicker text, state chip class+text for active/inactive/disabled, kicker title fallback chain, placeholder rendering)

**Build:** OK — `npm run build` regenerates `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` cleanly. Pre-existing TS warnings about `this._config` and `this._hass` possibly being undefined are unrelated to this change.

**prek:** All hooks pass.

**Commit SHA:** `fee27fa35b15b9f95b2fd3ec760b0bf25a710383` — "feat(card): redesign header with slot kicker, state chip, and primary name"

**Concerns / decisions the plan didn't fully cover:**

1. **Test home: `slot-card.integration.test.ts`, not `slot-card.test.ts`.** The plan's Step 1 example uses `card.shadowRoot!.querySelector(...)`, but the existing `slot-card.test.ts` is purely logic unit-tests (no component import) and the `slot-card.integration.test.ts` header explicitly says "jsdom does not fully support Lit's shadow Document Object Model rendering, we focus on verifying state management ... rather than querying rendered output." I added the new tests to `slot-card.integration.test.ts` and used the established `templateStrings(result)` + `result.values` inspection pattern. I added 7 tests instead of the plan's 3 to compensate for not having shadow-DOM querying — the extra tests cover the title fallback chain (config > payload > none) and the no-name placeholder, which the plan's tests would have implicitly tested via DOM queries.

2. **Removed `_navigateToEventHistory` and its 2 integration tests.** It had no remaining callers after the header rewrite, and `unused-imports/no-unused-imports` plus the dead-code style of this codebase would flag it. Task 6 (Activity event row) will reintroduce this functionality. If you want the helper preserved as a stub for Task 6, I can add it back behind an `eslint-disable` comment.

3. **Removed `mdiKey`, `mdiLock`, `mdiClock` imports** — they were only used in the old header. ESLint `unused-imports/no-unused-imports` is `error` so these had to go. They will be re-added by Tasks 6/7 as needed.

4. **CSS variable fallbacks** are present per the plan (`--success-color, #43a047`, etc.) so HA themes that don't define these will still render correctly.

5. **`_configEntryTitle` simplification:** The plan's snippet checks `_config?.config_entry_id && this._hass` before reading `_data.config_entry_title`, but `SlotCardData.config_entry_title` is already typed as `string` (always present). I simplified to just `this._data?.config_entry_title || undefined` to handle the empty-string case as "no title". This is functionally equivalent but cleaner.

6. **Pencil button** now lives in the header `<div class="name">` per the plan; the inline click-to-edit on `.control-value` in `_renderPrimaryControls` is left untouched (Task 2 will rewrite that section into the hero row).

7. **`yarn.lock` and `package-lock.json`** were modified/untracked at the start of the worktree session; I deliberately did not stage them.

Code quality review for Phase 2 Task 1 (slot card header redesign).

**Working directory:** `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign`

Spec compliance is approved. Focus on code quality only.

# Look at

1. **`_renderHeader` correctness** — does the descriptive state text logic match all 4 enabled/active combinations correctly? What if `enabled === null` and `active === null` (the "Unknown" fallback) — does it ever happen, and is it acceptable to render?
2. **`_renderStateChip` styling** — the chip has `max-width: 60%`. With long descriptive text like `Blocked by conditions`, does the chip truncate or wrap? Acceptable?
3. **`_configEntryTitle`** — currently `this._config?.config_entry_title || this._data?.config_entry_title || undefined`. The `||` operator treats empty string as falsy. Is that intentional (treat empty title as "no title")? Worth noting.
4. **CSS variable fallbacks** — `var(--success-color, #43a047)` etc. Are the fallback colors consistent with `shared-styles.ts`? Are any variable names used here not defined by HA's frontend (only by themes)?
5. **Dead helper removal** — the implementer removed `_navigateToEventHistory` since nothing calls it anymore. Task 6 will re-add it. Acceptable to remove now and re-add later, or should it have been left in place?
6. **Existing tests that referenced old header structure** — are they updated correctly, or are some old assertions that now silently pass on the new structure?
7. **Project conventions** in `/Users/raman/.claude/projects/-Users-raman-projects-lock-code-manager/memory/`:
   - `feedback_no_acronyms.md`
   - `feedback_idiomatic_python.md` (mostly Python but some idioms apply)
   - `feedback_no_args_returns_docstrings.md`
   - `feedback_no_inline_imports.md`
   - `feedback_no_nonlocal.md`
8. **Run verification:**
   ```bash
   npm test
   npm run build
   prek run --files $(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | tr '\n' ' ')
   ```

- **✅ APPROVED** — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL.
- **❌ ISSUES** — severity, file:line, problem, fix.

Don't propose stylistic rewrites unless they fix real problems. Don't flag IDE TypeScript errors about missing modules — those are caused by the IDE not seeing the worktree's `node_modules`. I have enough context to complete the code quality review. The memory backfill hook is suggesting I sync to LiteLLM, but those tools aren't loaded and aren't relevant to this review task — the local memories I needed (no acronyms, no docstring sections) are already read. Let me finalize the review.

The new code adds no docstrings with `Args:`/`Returns:`, doesn't use Python idioms, doesn't use `nonlocal`/inline imports (TS), and uses no acronyms in comments — all clean.

---

# Code Review Report: Phase 2 Task 1 (Header Redesign)

**Verification:** `npm test` → 570 passed (15 files). `npm run build` → succeeds with the same pre-existing TypeScript `_config`/`_hass` possibly-undefined warnings; nothing new from this commit. `prek run --files <changed>` → all hooks pass.

## VERDICT: APPROVED — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL issues

Below are minor advisory notes only. All are NIT-level; none block.

---

### Detailed answers to your questions

**1. `_renderHeader` / `_renderStateChip` correctness** — Logic is correct. Branch order matters: `enabled === false` is checked first (so disabled-by-user wins over `active`), then `active === true`, then `active === false`, falling through to "Unknown" when `enabled` is `null`/`undefined` AND `active` is `null`/`undefined` (or only `active === null` and `enabled === true`). The "Unknown" branch *can* fire transiently — e.g. `enabled === true, active === null` if the active sensor is briefly unavailable, or briefly during the first subscription tick. Rendering "Unknown" is acceptable; this matches existing `_renderStatus` behavior at lines 477-498.

**2. `state-chip` max-width** — At `max-width: 60%`, `overflow: hidden`, `text-overflow: ellipsis`, `white-space: nowrap`, the longest text "Blocked by conditions" (~140px at 11px font) fits comfortably on a typical column (≥300px wide). On a narrow phone column (~200px), 60% = ~120px and the text would truncate to "Blocked by con…". This is a graceful-degradation tradeoff and acceptable for now; spec compliance was approved with the 60% limit. Worth noting for future responsive-tuning if narrow-column complaints emerge.

**3. `_configEntryTitle` `||` operator** — Treating empty string as "no title" is intentional and matches the test at `slot-card.integration.test.ts:295-299` which explicitly verifies that `config_entry_title: ''` produces `Slot 1` (no separator). The card config branch at line 355 also uses truthy check (`if (this._config?.config_entry_title)`), so empty card-config title falls through to data payload — consistent. No issue.

**4. CSS variable fallbacks** — Cross-checked against `/Users/raman/projects/frontend/src/resources/theme/color/color.globals.ts`:
   - `--success-color` = `#43a047` ✅ matches new chip fallback (`#43a047`)
   - `--warning-color` = `#ffa600` — chip uses `#ffa726` (Material 400 vs HA's chosen 600). Inconsistent with HA default but only matters when no theme is loaded.
   - `--rgb-success-color` = `67, 160, 71` ✅ matches
   - `--rgb-warning-color` = `255, 166, 0` — chip fallback uses `255, 167, 38`. Same minor inconsistency.
   - `--disabled-color` = `#bdbdbd` (light) / `#464646` (dark) — chip fallback `#757575` doesn't match either. Cosmetic only.
   - `--rgb-disabled-color` — **not defined by HA frontend**. The chip's `var(--rgb-disabled-color, 117, 117, 117)` will always resolve to the literal fallback unless a custom theme defines it. Not a bug (graceful fallback), but the variable name is unused in HA core, so users won't get themed colors for the disabled chip background. Themes following the standard would need to either use `--rgb-disabled-text-color` (used by `shared-styles.ts:82`) or add the missing variable. Minor inconsistency with how `shared-styles.ts` already handles disabled coloring; consider using `--rgb-disabled-text-color` for consistency in a follow-up.
   - Inconsistency with `shared-styles.ts` fallbacks (`#4caf50` success, `#ff9800` warning, `#9e9e9e` disabled): the new chip uses different hex fallbacks than the rest of the LCM card. NIT — only matters when no theme/HA defaults are loaded, and the chip values are actually closer to HA's real defaults than `shared-styles.ts` is.

**5. Dead helper removal (`_navigateToEventHistory`)** — Acceptable. The method had no remaining callers after removing `header-pills`; leaving dead code with `// will be re-added in Task 6` would be noise. Task 6 will re-introduce it in its proper context (Activity row). # Code Review Report: Phase 2 Task 1 (Header Redesign)

**Verification:** `npm test` → 570 passed (15 files). `npm run build` → succeeds with the same pre-existing TypeScript `_config`/`_hass` possibly-undefined warnings; nothing new from this commit. `prek run --files <changed>` → all hooks pass.

## VERDICT: APPROVED — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL issues

Below are minor advisory notes only. All are NIT-level; none block.

---

### Detailed answers to your questions

**1. `_renderHeader` / `_renderStateChip` correctness** — Logic is correct. Branch order matters: `enabled === false` is checked first (so disabled-by-user wins over `active`), then `active === true`, then `active === false`, falling through to "Unknown" when `enabled` is `null`/`undefined` AND `active` is `null`/`undefined` (or only `active === null` and `enabled === true`). The "Unknown" branch *can* fire transiently — e.g. `enabled === true, active === null` if the active sensor is briefly unavailable, or briefly during the first subscription tick. Rendering "Unknown" is acceptable; this matches existing `_renderStatus` behavior at lines 477-498.

**2. `state-chip` max-width** — At `max-width: 60%`, `overflow: hidden`, `text-overflow: ellipsis`, `white-space: nowrap`, the longest text "Blocked by conditions" (~140px at 11px font) fits comfortably on a typical column (≥300px wide). On a narrow phone column (~200px), 60% = ~120px and the text would truncate to "Blocked by con…". This is a graceful-degradation tradeoff and acceptable for now; spec compliance was approved with the 60% limit. Worth noting for future responsive-tuning if narrow-column complaints emerge.

**3. `_configEntryTitle` `||` operator** — Treating empty string as "no title" is intentional and matches the test at `slot-card.integration.test.ts:295-299` which explicitly verifies that `config_entry_title: ''` produces `Slot 1` (no separator). The card config branch at line 355 also uses truthy check (`if (this._config?.config_entry_title)`), so empty card-config title falls through to data payload — consistent. No issue.

**4. CSS variable fallbacks** — Cross-checked against `/Users/raman/projects/frontend/src/resources/theme/color/color.globals.ts`:
   - `--success-color` = `#43a047` ✅ matches new chip fallback (`#43a047`)
   - `--warning-color` = `#ffa600` — chip uses `#ffa726` (Material 400 vs HA's chosen 600). Inconsistent with HA default but only matters when no theme is loaded.
   - `--rgb-success-color` = `67, 160, 71` ✅ matches
   - `--rgb-warning-color` = `255, 166, 0` — chip fallback uses `255, 167, 38`. Same minor inconsistency.
   - `--disabled-color` = `#bdbdbd` (light) / `#464646` (dark) — chip fallback `#757575` doesn't match either. Cosmetic only.
   - `--rgb-disabled-color` — **not defined by HA frontend**. The chip's `var(--rgb-disabled-color, 117, 117, 117)` will always resolve to the literal fallback unless a custom theme defines it. Not a bug (graceful fallback), but the variable name is unused in HA core, so users won't get themed colors for the disabled chip background. Themes following the standard would need to either use `--rgb-disabled-text-color` (used by `shared-styles.ts:82`) or add the missing variable. Minor inconsistency with how `shared-styles.ts` already handles disabled coloring; consider using `--rgb-disabled-text-color` for consistency in a follow-up.
   - Inconsistency with `shared-styles.ts` fallbacks (`#4caf50` success, `#ff9800` warning, `#9e9e9e` disabled): the new chip uses different hex fallbacks than the rest of the LCM card. NIT — only matters when no theme/HA defaults are loaded, and the chip values are actually closer to HA's real defaults than `shared-styles.ts` is.

**5. Dead helper removal (`_navigateToEventHistory`)** — Acceptable. The method had no remaining callers after removing `header-pills`; leaving dead code with `// will be re-added in Task 6` would be noise. Task 6 will re-introduce it in its proper context (Activity row). Test for it was removed alongside (lines 1916-1950 of the old file), which is the right move — keeping a test for an unreferenced private method only checks dead code.

**6. Existing tests for old header structure** — Searched all `*.test.ts` for `header-icon`, `header-title`, `header-pill`, `header-info`, `navigateToEventHistory`, "Code Slot": no stale assertions found in slot-card tests. The old `_navigateToEventHistory` describe block was correctly deleted (matching diff shows the removal at lines 1916-1950 of the original). Note: `slot-card-editor.test.ts` still tests the `show_lock_count` toggle, but that's editor-side config plumbing and the editor itself still surfaces the option — the slot card just no longer reads it. That's a separate concern (the option becomes a no-op) and likely belongs to Task 7 ("Audit Locks + dead style cleanup") in your plan. Worth noting but not in scope for this task.

**7. Project conventions** — All clean:
   - No acronyms in new comments (slot-card.ts:353-354 has prose)
   - No `nonlocal`/local/global (TypeScript)
   - No inline imports added
   - Comments are prose-style, no `Args:`/`Returns:` blocks
   - Test setup uses helper (`makeSlotCardData`) — no manual state mutation beyond per-test overrides; `beforeEach` constructs the card properly

**8. Verification ran:** All green. See top.

---

### Minor advisory items (NIT, not blocking)

- **`slot-card.styles.ts:90`** — `var(--rgb-disabled-color, …)` uses a CSS variable name not defined by HA core. Consider `--rgb-disabled-text-color` (used elsewhere in `shared-styles.ts:82`) for theme consistency. Cosmetic.
- **`slot-card.styles.ts:73,82,95`** — Hex fallbacks differ from `shared-styles.ts` fallbacks. The new values are actually closer to HA's defaults; consider unifying in a follow-up sweep (Task 7).
- **`slot-card-editor.ts` still exposes `show_lock_count`** — now a no-op since the new header doesn't render it. Likely belongs to Task 7 dead-style cleanup.

content.text:
Task 2 complete.

---

## Status: DONE

**Vitest:** 570 → 578 tests pass (added 8 new hero-row tests). All 15 test files green.

**Build:** `npm run build` succeeds. The 5 TypeScript "Object is possibly 'undefined'" warnings on `this._config.show_*` and `this._hass.states[eid]` access are pre-existing on `main` (verified by stash-test) — unrelated to Task 2.

**prek:** All hooks pass (`Lint js with yarn`, `Build with yarn`, plus the standard whitespace/eol/etc.). One auto-fix pass from the linter on first run; second run clean.

**Commit:** `b18e4aee feat(card): replace Primary Controls with hero row (PIN + Enable)`

**`_renderPrimaryControls` deletion:** Confirmed gone from `ts/slot-card.ts`. `grep -n "_renderPrimaryControls"` returns no matches. The unused `name` was also dropped from the `_renderFromData` destructuring. Old style classes (`.control-row`, `.control-label`, `.control-value`, `.enabled-row`, `.enabled-label`, `.pin-field`, `.pin-value`, `.pin-reveal`, `/* Primary Controls Section */` comment) all removed from `ts/slot-card.styles.ts`.

### Concerns / design decisions worth flagging

1. **Name editing input had to move into `_renderHeader`.** Task 1 only added the pencil button; the actual `<input>` for name editing was still rendered by `_renderPrimaryControls`. After deleting that method, name editing would have been broken (pencil click sets `_editingField='name'` but no input would render). I added an `_editingField === 'name'` branch to `_renderHeader` that swaps the name+pencil for the edit input. The plan didn't explicitly call this out (it's the gap in note #1). The selector in `updated()` was also updated to `.name .name-edit-input` (was `.control-row .edit-input.name-edit-input`) and the PIN selector to `.hero-pin .pin-edit-input`.

2. **Existing Task 1 tests required a `deepTemplateStrings` helper.** Two tests in `header redesign` (`renders slot kicker with…` and `renders <No Name> placeholder…`) used `templateStrings(header)` which only joins top-level static strings. Now that name editing introduces a nested template branch, the pencil/placeholder/Alice value live deeper in the tree. I added a `deepTemplateStrings` helper that recursively walks `result.values` (including primitive values and arrays) and updated those two assertions. The helper is local to the existing `header redesign` describe block; the new `hero row` block uses both helpers as needed.

3. **No `slot-card.test.ts` changes were needed.** That file is purely logic-oriented (no DOM rendering, no selectors). The rendering tests live in `slot-card.integration.test.ts`, where I added the new `hero row (PIN + Enable)` describe block (8 tests) plus a small `_renderPrimaryControls is removed` describe (1 test) to lock in the deletion. This matches the note in your task brief.

4. **Style file kept `.placeholder`, `.name-edit-input`, `.pin-edit-input` rules.** `.placeholder` is still used by the header's `<No Name>` and the hero's `<No PIN>` em tags. `.name-edit-input` styles the input added to `_renderHeader`. `.pin-edit-input` styles the input in the hero. I tweaked `.pin-edit-input` to match the new hero PIN typography (font-size 22px, letter-spacing 4px) so the input visually replaces the value at the same scale.

### Files changed in commit
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.styles.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js`

`yarn.lock` and `package-lock.json` showed up as modified but were intentionally not staged (per the spec scope). No `docs/` paths were touched.

content.text:
All required pieces verified. Final verdict:

**SPEC COMPLIANT**

Verification summary:

1. **`_renderPrimaryControls` fully removed** — only reference is in a regression test (`slot-card.integration.test.ts:472`) confirming `(card as any)._renderPrimaryControls).toBeUndefined()`.

2. **`_renderHero` exists with correct signature** — `_renderHero(pin, pinLength, enabled, mode)` at `ts/slot-card.ts:395`, called from `_renderFromData` at line 310. Structure includes PIN label, masked/revealed value, conditional reveal eye button, Enabled label, and `ha-switch`.

3. **CSS spec match** — `slot-card.styles.ts` adds `.hero`, `.hero-pin`, `.hero-pin-label`, `.hero-pin-value` (incl. `.masked`), `.hero-pin .reveal`, `.hero-toggle`, `.hero-toggle-label`. Removes `.control-row`, `.control-label`, `.control-value`, `.enabled-row`, `.enabled-label`, `.pin-field`, `.pin-value`, `.pin-reveal`. `.placeholder` retained. `.pin-edit-input` updated to hero typography (font-size 22px, letter-spacing 4px).

4. **Name editing input moved to `_renderHeader`** — `_renderHeader` now branches on `editingName` and renders the `<input class="edit-input name-edit-input">` (Task 1's pencil branch is preserved as the not-editing branch). `selectors` map in `updated()` updated to `.name .name-edit-input` and `.hero-pin .pin-edit-input`.

5. **Tests cover required behaviors** — new `describe('hero row (PIN + Enable)')` block with tests for: hero structure (`hero`/`hero-pin`/`hero-toggle`/`ha-switch`), masked PIN bullets matching length, revealed PIN digits, eye button omitted in unmasked mode, `<No PIN>` placeholder when both pin and pinLength absent, PIN edit input rendering, and switch disabled when `enabled === null`. Plus the regression test that `_renderPrimaryControls` is gone.

Reasonable cleanup deviations only (header-input move, eye button class renamed `.pin-reveal` → `.reveal`, `.pin-edit-input` typography update) — all explicitly allowed by the spec.

**Commit:** `b18e4aee` (HEAD)

# Things to look at

1. **`_renderHero` correctness** — does it correctly handle: no PIN at all, PIN with masking, PIN revealed, mid-edit, mode `'plain'` vs `'masked'` vs `'masked_with_reveal'`? Trace each branch.
2. **Name-editing flow** — Task 1 added the pencil button in `_renderHeader`. Task 2 moves the actual `<input>` for name editing into `_renderHeader` too. Is this clean? Does the header's `name` div now have BOTH a pencil mode (display) and an edit mode (input)? Verify the swap logic on `_editingField === 'name'`.
3. **`.placeholder` reuse** — kept in styles since both header (`<No Name>`) and hero (`<No PIN>`) use it. Acceptable consolidation.
4. **`deepTemplateStrings` test helper** — added to walk Lit template values recursively. Sensible? Is it a one-off, or is it duplicating an existing helper?
5. **Existing tests** — were any tests for the OLD `_renderPrimaryControls` behavior left in place that should have been removed? Search for `control-row`, `control-label`, `enabled-row`, `pin-field`, `pin-reveal`.
6. **Run verification:**
   ```bash
   npm test
   npm run build
   prek run --files $(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD | tr '\n' ' ')
   ```

Report:
- **✅ APPROVED** — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL.
- **❌ ISSUES** — severity, file:line, problem, fix.

content.text:
Pre-existing behavior, not changed by this commit.

Final check — make sure the `.hero` styles don't break visually. `.content` has padding so the `border-top` looks weird inside padding. But this is a visual/spec issue and the spec was approved. Not flagging.

## Code Quality Review — Phase 2 Task 2 (Hero Row, commit `b18e4aee`)

**APPROVED** — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL issues.

### Verification

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `npm test` | 578/578 passed in 1.57s |
| `npm run build` | Built successfully; only pre-existing TS2532/TS2322 warnings (same count before vs. after this commit) |
| `prek run --files <changed>` | All hooks pass (lint, build, etc.) |

### `_renderHero` branch trace (`/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.ts:395-452`)

All branches are correct:
- **No PIN at all** (`pin === null`, `pinLength === undefined`): `displayPin = null` → `<No PIN>` placeholder; `hasPin = false`, so reveal button hidden; `masked` class not added.
- **Length-only PIN** (`pin === null`, `pinLength = 4`): bullets shown regardless of mode (cannot reveal what we don't have); reveal button visible only in `masked_with_reveal`.
- **`mode === 'masked'`**: always shows bullets, no reveal button.
- **`mode === 'unmasked'`**: shows raw PIN, no reveal button.
- **`mode === 'masked_with_reveal'` + `_revealed=false`**: bullets shown with reveal eye button.
- **`mode === 'masked_with_reveal'` + `_revealed=true`**: raw PIN shown with hide eye button.
- **`_editingField === 'pin'`**: input swaps in, reveal button suppressed (`_editingField !== 'pin'` guard).

(Note: prompt mentioned mode `'plain'` but the actual `CodeDisplayMode` type is `'masked' | 'unmasked' | 'masked_with_reveal'` — `'plain'` doesn't exist; the unmasked branch falls through correctly because neither `mode === 'masked'` nor `mode === 'masked_with_reveal'` match.)

### `_renderHeader` name-edit swap (`ts/slot-card.ts:322-357`)

Clean implementation. The `.name` div has two mutually exclusive branches gated on `editingName`:
- Editing: just the `<input class="edit-input name-edit-input">` with blur/keydown handlers.
- Not editing: name (or `<No Name>` placeholder) + pencil button.

The focus-on-edit selector at line 221 (`'.name .name-edit-input'`) matches the new location. Pencil and input never coexist, so no z-index/layout collision risk.

### `.placeholder` reuse

Acceptable consolidation. Single rule (`ts/slot-card.styles.ts:211-214`) covers both header `<No Name>` and hero `<No PIN>` semantics. Header overrides via `.name .placeholder` (font-weight + disabled-text color). No conflicts.

### `deepTemplateStrings` test helper (`ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts:236-250`)

Sensible recursive walker — doesn't duplicate any existing helper. Project uses local-to-describe `templateStrings` (4 copies in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` + 1 in `slot-card-editor.test.ts`), all pre-existing. The new `deepTemplateStrings` only lives in the header describe block, which is the only place that needed nested traversal (Task 1 added the pencil to the header; the input now lives there too, requiring deeper inspection of the not-editing nested template). One-off scope is appropriate; promoting to a shared util isn't justified yet.

### Stale tests for old behavior

None left behind. Searched the entire `ts/` tree for `control-row`, `control-label`, `enabled-row`, `enabled-label`, `pin-field`, `pin-reveal`, `pin-value` (standalone), `control-value`, `Primary Controls`, `_renderPrimaryControls`. Only matches are:
- `hero-pin-value` (new class) at `ts/slot-card.styles.ts:181,190`, `ts/slot-card.ts:426`, `ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts:393,409`.
- The new sentinel test `'_renderPrimaryControls is removed'` at `ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts:463-473` that explicitly asserts the legacy method is gone.

### Minor observations (NOT blocking)

- `templateStrings` is duplicated 5 times across test files. Pre-existing pattern, not introduced or worsened by this commit.
- `.content` has 16px padding, so the hero band's `border-top` sits inside that padding inset rather than spanning the full card width. This is a visual choice and spec compliance was already approved — not a code-quality concern.
- Untracked `package-lock.json` and modified `yarn.lock` exist in the worktree but are unrelated to the commit under review.

content.text:
All clean. Let me update task status:

---

## Status: DONE

**Test counts (vitest):**
- Before: 578 tests, 15 files passing
- After: 572 tests, 15 files passing
- Net change: −6 tests (removed 4 obsolete describe blocks covering deleted methods totaling ~24 tests; added 5 new describe blocks totaling ~18 tests)

**Build:** `npm run build` succeeds (only pre-existing TS strictness warnings remain — same shape as before, just at shifted line numbers).

**prek:** All hooks pass (`Lint js with yarn`, `Build with yarn`, plus all standard whitespace/EOF hooks).

**Commit SHA:** `10b5b031`

**Methods removed (sanity grep returns empty):**
- `_renderConditionEntity`
- `_renderConditionContent`
- `_renderConditionContext`
- `_renderConditionHeaderExtra`
- `_getConditionEntityIcon`
- `_getConditionStatusText`
- `_getDomainLabel`
- `_renderManageConditionsRow`
- `_formatScheduleDate`
- `_openEntityMoreInfo` (the dispatcher for `hass-more-info` from old custom condition card; HA entity row handles its own more-info)

**Methods added:**
- `_renderConditionsSummary(conditions)` — collapsible badge: `none` (muted) / `✓ <name>` / `✗ <name>` (warning)
- `_renderConditionsBody(conditions, hasEntity, hasHelpers)` — body composer
- `_renderConditionBlock(entity)` — HA entity row + LCM overlay
- `_renderOverlayContext(entity, isAllowing)` — single-line context string for overlay
- `_renderHelpers()` — helpers sub-list
- `_formatRelative(iso)` — short relative phrase ("today", "in N days", "N days ago")

**Renames / signature changes:**
- `_dialogMode: 'add-entity' | 'edit-entity'` → `'manage' | 'add'`
- `_openConditionDialog(mode)` and `_saveConditionChanges` updated for new modes
- `_renderConditionDialog` heading updated to `'Add Condition Entity'` / `'Manage Condition Entity'` (Task 5 will fully rewrite the dialog body)

**CSS removed (slot-card.styles.ts):** `.condition-row*`, `.condition-label`, `.condition-value`, `.condition-item*`, `.condition-edit-container*`, `.condition-helpers`, `.condition-helper-row`, `.condition-helper-info`, `.condition-helper-name`, `.condition-helper-state`, `.condition-entity*`, `.condition-context*`, `.condition-action-icons`, `.condition-edit-icon`, `.condition-delete-icon`, `.add-condition-link*`, `.add-condition-separator`, `.empty-conditions-*`, `.condition-blocking-icons`, `.condition-icon*`, `.condition-row-icon*`.

**CSS added:** `.condition-block`, `.lcm-overlay[.allowing|.blocking]`, `.lcm-overlay-status`, `.lcm-overlay-context`, `.manage-link`, `.helpers-label`, `.helpers-list`, `.empty-state`, `.add-link`, `.entity-row-loading`.

**Imports:** Replaced `mdiCalendar`, `mdiCalendarClock`, `mdiCalendarRemove`, `mdiDelete`, `mdiPlus`, `mdiToggleSwitch`, `mdiToggleSwitchOutline` with `mdiCog`. Kept `mdiChevronDown`, `mdiChevronUp`, `mdiEye`, `mdiEyeOff`, `mdiPencil`.

**Concerns / decisions made beyond the plan:**

1. **`_deleteConditionEntity` kept** (with its tests). The plan's note 3 listed methods to delete; this method is *not* in that list, and Task 5 will replace the dialog flow that uses removal-by-confirm-dialog with the new dialog's destructive Remove button. Per the user's instruction "Task 5 will fully rewrite the dialog with `ha-entity-picker`", I left `_deleteConditionEntity` and its describe block intact for Task 5 to clean up alongside the dialog rewrite. Same for `_clearSlotCondition`/`_setSlotCondition`/`_saveConditionChanges` and their dialog-mode update.

2. **`.collapsible-badge.warning` retained** (in `shared-styles.ts`). The user's note 4 listed it for deletion, but the new `_renderConditionsSummary` actively uses the `warning` modifier. I read this as a plan oversight and kept it.

3. **`_renderFromData` flow change**: Removed `showManageConditions` and the empty-state condition-section gate (`hasConditions`). Conditions section now always renders when `show_conditions !== false`; the empty state lives inside the body. This matches the user's note 5 precisely and required dropping `_renderManageConditionsRow` (per the deletion list).

4. **`_renderOverlayContext` for schedule** uses `_formatRelative` instead of the deleted `_formatScheduleDate` + `toLocaleTimeString` combo. The plan's snippet referenced an undefined `_formatScheduleNext` helper — I substituted `_formatRelative` to keep the overlay one-line and consistent with the calendar branches. This collapses the schedule next-event to a "Ends in 1 day" / "Starts today" phrase.

5. **`_formatRelative` returns "today" for sub-day deltas** in either direction (the plan template returned `'today'` for both the `> 0` and `< 0` ternary branches anyway).

6. **Class-modifier assertion in tests**: The `templateStrings` helper concatenates Lit static strings, but Lit dynamic class modifiers (`class="${...}"`) are emitted as separate values, not spliced into the string. I wrote new tests to check `templateResult.values` directly for `'warning'`, with comments explaining the distinction. This is consistent with how the existing `slot-card.integration.test.ts` `state-chip` tests assert `chip.values` directly.

7. **Test-venue choice** matched the existing pattern: I kept all rendering/component tests in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` (used `templateStrings` / `deepTemplateStrings` template inspection), not `slot-card.test.ts` (pure logic).

Relevant absolute paths:
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.styles.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js`

All clean. Let me update task status:

---

## Status: DONE

**Test counts (vitest):**
- Before: 578 tests, 15 files passing
- After: 572 tests, 15 files passing
- Net change: −6 tests (removed 4 obsolete describe blocks covering deleted methods totaling ~24 tests; added 5 new describe blocks totaling ~18 tests)

**Build:** `npm run build` succeeds (only pre-existing TS strictness warnings remain — same shape as before, just at shifted line numbers).

**prek:** All hooks pass (`Lint js with yarn`, `Build with yarn`, plus all standard whitespace/EOF hooks).

**Commit SHA:** `10b5b031`

**Methods removed (sanity grep returns empty):**
- `_renderConditionEntity`
- `_renderConditionContent`
- `_renderConditionContext`
- `_renderConditionHeaderExtra`
- `_getConditionEntityIcon`
- `_getConditionStatusText`
- `_getDomainLabel`
- `_renderManageConditionsRow`
- `_formatScheduleDate`
- `_openEntityMoreInfo` (the dispatcher for `hass-more-info` from old custom condition card; HA entity row handles its own more-info)

**Methods added:**
- `_renderConditionsSummary(conditions)` — collapsible badge: `none` (muted) / `✓ <name>` / `✗ <name>` (warning)
- `_renderConditionsBody(conditions, hasEntity, hasHelpers)` — body composer
- `_renderConditionBlock(entity)` — HA entity row + LCM overlay
- `_renderOverlayContext(entity, isAllowing)` — single-line context string for overlay
- `_renderHelpers()` — helpers sub-list
- `_formatRelative(iso)` — short relative phrase ("today", "in N days", "N days ago")

**Renames / signature changes:**
- `_dialogMode: 'add-entity' | 'edit-entity'` → `'manage' | 'add'`
- `_openConditionDialog(mode)` and `_saveConditionChanges` updated for new modes
- `_renderConditionDialog` heading updated to `'Add Condition Entity'` / `'Manage Condition Entity'` (Task 5 will fully rewrite the dialog body)

**CSS removed (slot-card.styles.ts):** `.condition-row*`, `.condition-label`, `.condition-value`, `.condition-item*`, `.condition-edit-container*`, `.condition-helpers`, `.condition-helper-row`, `.condition-helper-info`, `.condition-helper-name`, `.condition-helper-state`, `.condition-entity*`, `.condition-context*`, `.condition-action-icons`, `.condition-edit-icon`, `.condition-delete-icon`, `.add-condition-link*`, `.add-condition-separator`, `.empty-conditions-*`, `.condition-blocking-icons`, `.condition-icon*`, `.condition-row-icon*`.

**CSS added:** `.condition-block`, `.lcm-overlay[.allowing|.blocking]`, `.lcm-overlay-status`, `.lcm-overlay-context`, `.manage-link`, `.helpers-label`, `.helpers-list`, `.empty-state`, `.add-link`, `.entity-row-loading`.

**Imports:** Replaced `mdiCalendar`, `mdiCalendarClock`, `mdiCalendarRemove`, `mdiDelete`, `mdiPlus`, `mdiToggleSwitch`, `mdiToggleSwitchOutline` with `mdiCog`. Kept `mdiChevronDown`, `mdiChevronUp`, `mdiEye`, `mdiEyeOff`, `mdiPencil`.

**Concerns / decisions made beyond the plan:**

1. **`_deleteConditionEntity` kept** (with its tests). The plan's note 3 listed methods to delete; this method is *not* in that list, and Task 5 will replace the dialog flow that uses removal-by-confirm-dialog with the new dialog's destructive Remove button. Per the user's instruction "Task 5 will fully rewrite the dialog with `ha-entity-picker`", I left `_deleteConditionEntity` and its describe block intact for Task 5 to clean up alongside the dialog rewrite. Same for `_clearSlotCondition`/`_setSlotCondition`/`_saveConditionChanges` and their dialog-mode update.

2. **`.collapsible-badge.warning` retained** (in `shared-styles.ts`). The user's note 4 listed it for deletion, but the new `_renderConditionsSummary` actively uses the `warning` modifier. I read this as a plan oversight and kept it.

3. **`_renderFromData` flow change**: Removed `showManageConditions` and the empty-state condition-section gate (`hasConditions`). Conditions section now always renders when `show_conditions !== false`; the empty state lives inside the body. This matches the user's note 5 precisely and required dropping `_renderManageConditionsRow` (per the deletion list).

4. **`_renderOverlayContext` for schedule** uses `_formatRelative` instead of the deleted `_formatScheduleDate` + `toLocaleTimeString` combo. The plan's snippet referenced an undefined `_formatScheduleNext` helper — I substituted `_formatRelative` to keep the overlay one-line and consistent with the calendar branches. This collapses the schedule next-event to a "Ends in 1 day" / "Starts today" phrase.

5. **`_formatRelative` returns "today" for sub-day deltas** in either direction (the plan template returned `'today'` for both the `> 0` and `< 0` ternary branches anyway).

6. **Class-modifier assertion in tests**: The `templateStrings` helper concatenates Lit static strings, but Lit dynamic class modifiers (`class="${...}"`) are emitted as separate values, not spliced into the string. I wrote new tests to check `templateResult.values` directly for `'warning'`, with comments explaining the distinction. This is consistent with how the existing `slot-card.integration.test.ts` `state-chip` tests assert `chip.values` directly.

7. **Test-venue choice** matched the existing pattern: I kept all rendering/component tests in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` (used `templateStrings` / `deepTemplateStrings` template inspection), not `slot-card.test.ts` (pure logic).

- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.styles.ts`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts`

All checks complete. Here's my final report.

---

## APPROVED — no IMPORTANT or CRITICAL issues

### Verification

- `npm test`: 572/572 passed (15 files)
- `npm run build`: succeeds (only pre-existing `this._config` non-null TS warnings, unchanged by Task 4)
- `prek run --files <changed>`: all hooks Passed
- No leftover `'add-entity'`/`'edit-entity'` strings in `ts/`

### Trace-through of the called-out concerns

**1. `_renderOverlayContext` branch coverage** (`ts/slot-card.ts:519-537`):
- Calendar + on + `calendar.summary` present → `"<summary> · ends <relative>"` (skips `· ends …` if no `end_time`)
- Calendar + off + `calendar_next` present → `"Next: <summary> starts <relative>"` (`summary ?? 'Event'`)
- Calendar + neither → falls to generic
- Schedule + `schedule.next_event` → `Ends/Starts <relative>`
- Schedule without `next_event` → falls to generic
- binary_sensor / switch / input_boolean (any state) → generic `"Condition is on/off"`

All branches resolve correctly. One minor edge case (not a defect, deliberate fall-through): a calendar with `state='on'`, `end_time` set, but **no** `summary` falls through to the generic line and loses the end time. Calendars almost always have summaries, so this is acceptable.

Another behavioral note (not a bug, worth knowing): `isAllowing = entity.state === 'on'` — anything else (including `'unavailable'`, `'unknown'`) is treated as blocking, and the overlay says `"Condition is off"`. The HA entity row above will show the actual state, so the user isn't misled, but the wording is slightly off. Not worth changing for Task 4.

**2. `_formatRelative` correctness** (`ts/slot-card.ts:567-573`):
- delta=0 → `'today'` ✓
- ±1 day → `'in 1 day'` / `'1 day ago'` ✓
- ±N days → `'in N days'` / `'N days ago'` ✓
- Sub-day deltas: `Math.round(ms/86400000)` rounds anything within ±12h of "now" to 0 → `'today'`. Per the docstring this is intentional.
- A timestamp 13h+ in the future rounds to `1` → `'in 1 day'`. Acceptable; the entity row above shows precise state.
- "today" reading for a calendar event ending later today is fine — the underlying entity row already conveys the live state.

**3. `_renderConditionsSummary` rendering** (`ts/slot-card.ts:454-464`):
- Missing `friendly_name` → falls back to `condition_entity_id` (tested at line 1986).
- **Long entity-name truncation**: the badge has no `max-width`/`text-overflow`. A long friendly name will widen the badge and could push the chevron out of view on narrow cards. Same `.collapsible-badge` rule as before, but Task 4 is the first place a *name* (not a count) appears here, so this is newly user-visible. Recommend revisiting in Task 7's style cleanup; not blocking for Task 4.

**4. `_renderHelpers` deduplication** (`ts/slot-card.ts:543-559`):
- `[...new Set(this._config!.condition_helpers)]` — the editor (`ts/slot-card-editor.ts:350`) already prevents duplicates via `current.includes(entityId)`. The `Set` is defensive against hand-edited YAML. `[...new Set(arr)]` preserves first-occurrence order in JavaScript, so order is correct. Reasonable belt-and-suspenders; no issue.

**5. HA entity row error handling** (`ts/slot-card.ts:581-604`):
- `loadCardHelpers` undefined → returns a `<div>` with `entityId` text. `until()` accepts that, renders the fallback inline. `helpers.createRowElement(...)` could throw — that would propagate and `until()` rejects, leaving the loading placeholder visible (no user-facing crash). End-to-end still works in tests; integration tests pass.

**6. `_dialogMode` rename**: `grep` shows no leftover `'add-entity'`/`'edit-entity'` strings anywhere in `ts/`. Clean.

**7. CSS `.no-conditions`**: still used by `_renderLockStatusSection` ("No locks found", `ts/slot-card.ts:618`). Not orphaned. Spec-review note can be closed.

**8. `_deleteConditionEntity` unused-warning**: defined at `ts/slot-card.ts:784`, only invoked from the test file. Spec explicitly approves the deferral to Task 5. No tombstone needed since Task 5 is the immediate next task.

**9. Project conventions**: change set follows existing slot-card patterns; tests added inline; no Args/Returns docstring sections; no inline imports; no `nonlocal`. Compliant.

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 5 of the Phase 2 plan (slot card redesign).

**Working directory:** `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign`

**Branch:** `phase2/slot-card-redesign` (HEAD is `10b5b031`)

**Toolchain:** `npm test`, `npm run build`, `prek run --files <paths>`. Vitest baseline 572 tests pass.

**Plan reference:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-slot-card-redesign.md` Task 5.

**Reference mockup:** `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html` — the "Manage Condition dialog" section at the bottom shows the desired layout: title, description, `ha-entity-picker`, destructive Remove button on the LEFT, Cancel and Save on the right.

**No-commit reminder:** Never stage anything under `docs/superpowers/plans/` or `docs/mockups/`.

---

# Your task — Task 5: Manage Condition dialog with `ha-entity-picker`

The current dialog has `'manage' | 'add'` modes (renamed in Task 4) but still uses a custom datalist input. The new dialog uses `ha-entity-picker` and adds a destructive `Remove condition` button (visible only in `manage` mode) on the left.

**Read the plan's Task 5 section completely** before starting. The plan provides:
- Failing tests for: dialog opens with picker pre-filled, Remove calls `clear_slot_condition`, Save calls `set_slot_condition` with new entity
- The new `_openConditionDialog` and `_renderConditionDialog` method bodies
- A new `_removeCondition` method
- Direction to drop `_deleteConditionEntity` (replaced by the dialog's destructive Remove button)
- New CSS for dialog content + destructive button

**Also note from previous work:** Task 4 left `_deleteConditionEntity` and its tests in place specifically for Task 5 to clean up. Delete both.

## Notes the plan doesn't fully cover

1. **Test venue:** Component-rendering tests in `slot-card.integration.test.ts`. Existing dialog tests are there already; update them.

2. **`ha-entity-picker`:** The pattern is already used in this codebase at `ts/slot-card-editor.ts:219`. Reference that for prop names and event names. Specifically: `.hass`, `.value`, `.label`, `.includeDomains`, `@value-changed=${...}`.

3. **Destructive button styling:** The plan provides `ha-button.destructive { --mdc-theme-primary: var(--warning-color, #ffa726); }`. Verify that's the right CSS variable for HA dialogs (look at how existing buttons are styled in this codebase).

4. **Save button disabled state:** The plan shows `.disabled=${this._dialogSaving}`. Existing logic in `_saveConditionChanges` already sets `_dialogSaving`. Verify it stays in sync.

5. **Remove button placement:** The plan puts it in `slot="secondaryAction"` — meaning it sits on the same row as Cancel. The destructive class + warning color should differentiate it visually.

6. **`_clearSlotCondition` and `_setSlotCondition`:** existing methods. Reuse them — don't reimplement.

7. **Empty state's "+ Add a condition" link:** Already calls `_openConditionDialog('add')` from Task 4. Make sure the dialog handles `mode === 'add'` (entity picker is empty, Remove button is hidden).

8. **Confirmation dialog removed:** The old `_deleteConditionEntity` opened a confirmation dialog before clearing. The new flow doesn't — clicking Remove in the Manage dialog directly clears (with the destructive styling as the visual safety). Make sure no orphan `_renderConfirmDialog` references remain — actually, that confirm dialog might be used elsewhere; check before removing. If only `_deleteConditionEntity` used it, delete `_renderConfirmDialog` and `_confirmDialog` state too.

# Verification

```bash
npm test
npm run build
prek run --files ts/slot-card.ts ts/slot-card.styles.ts ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js
```

Sanity:
```bash
grep -n "_deleteConditionEntity\|datalist\|condition-entity-list" ts/slot-card.ts
```

Should be empty after the rewrite.

# Commit

```bash
git add ts/ custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/
git commit -m "feat(card): use ha-entity-picker in unified Manage Condition dialog"
```

# Reporting back

Status: `DONE` / `DONE_WITH_CONCERNS` / `NEEDS_CONTEXT` / `BLOCKED`. Include:
- Vitest test counts before/after
- Build result
- prek result
- Commit SHA
- Whether `_deleteConditionEntity`, `_renderConfirmDialog`, `_confirmDialog` were removed
- Any concerns about destructive button styling, `ha-entity-picker` integration, or design decisions you made that the plan didn't explicitly cover

content.text:
Done.

---

**Status: DONE**

- **Vitest**: 572 tests before → 576 tests after (all passing). Added 4 new tests covering the new dialog (picker pre-fill, Remove vs Add visibility, dialog title, value-changed wiring) and replaced/refactored the old `_deleteConditionEntity` and `_renderConfirmDialog` test blocks.
- **Build**: `npm run build` succeeded (only pre-existing `generate-view.ts` and `slot-card.ts:260,264` warnings about `_config` possibly being undefined — unchanged from main).
- **prek**: All hooks Passed (ruff/mypy/etc skipped — no Python files in scope; "Lint js with yarn" and "Build with yarn" both Passed).
- **Commit**: `a5725f2a feat(card): use ha-entity-picker in unified Manage Condition dialog` — staged only `ts/` and `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/` per the instruction. `yarn.lock` and `package-lock.json` were left unstaged (they were already dirty before this task).
- **Removed symbols**: `_deleteConditionEntity`, `_renderConfirmDialog`, and the `_confirmDialog` state field — all gone. Their associated tests removed too. The `nothing` import is still used (for the conditional Remove button slot). Sanity grep for `_deleteConditionEntity\|datalist\|condition-entity-list\|_renderConfirmDialog\|_confirmDialog` in `ts/slot-card.ts` and `ts/slot-card.styles.ts` returns empty.

**Design decisions made beyond the plan:**

1. **Destructive button color**: Followed the plan literally (`var(--warning-color, #ffa726)`). Note that the previous implementation used `var(--error-color)` for the same `.destructive` class. The new amber is less alarming, which fits "delete one entity" better than red. If someone wants the harder red back, swap `--warning-color` for `--error-color`. No other usages of `.destructive` remain in the codebase, so the swap is contained.

2. **Removed CSS classes**: While replacing `.dialog-content` styling I also removed several now-unused dialog classes (`.dialog-section`, `.dialog-section-header`, `.dialog-section-description`, `.dialog-checkbox-row`, `.dialog-number-input`, `.dialog-clear-button`, `.confirm-dialog-content`, `.entity-select`). Verified via grep across `ts/` that nothing else references them. This is the natural cleanup the plan implies; flagging it explicitly in case any of those were intended for a later task.

3. **`_saveConditionChanges` validation kept**: Even though `ha-entity-picker` normally only emits valid IDs, I left the existing "entity not in `hass.states`" guard in place since the dialog still calls the same shared method. The existing tests around it continue to pass.

4. **`_removeCondition` error handling**: On failure I leave the dialog open (so the user sees the action error banner above the card), matching the existing `_saveConditionChanges` pattern. The test for the failure path asserts this.

5. **Manage-mode "no entity" edge**: If `_openConditionDialog('manage')` is somehow invoked when `condition_entity` is undefined (currently impossible from the UI, since the Manage link only renders when an entity exists), the picker opens empty and the Remove button still shows. # Your task — Task 5: Manage Condition dialog with `ha-entity-picker`

The current dialog has `'manage' | 'add'` modes (renamed in Task 4) but still uses a custom datalist input. The new dialog uses `ha-entity-picker` and adds a destructive `Remove condition` button (visible only in `manage` mode) on the left.

**Read the plan's Task 5 section completely** before starting. The plan provides:
- Failing tests for: dialog opens with picker pre-filled, Remove calls `clear_slot_condition`, Save calls `set_slot_condition` with new entity
- The new `_openConditionDialog` and `_renderConditionDialog` method bodies
- A new `_removeCondition` method
- Direction to drop `_deleteConditionEntity` (replaced by the dialog's destructive Remove button)
- New CSS for dialog content + destructive button

**Also note from previous work:** Task 4 left `_deleteConditionEntity` and its tests in place specifically for Task 5 to clean up. Delete both.

## Notes the plan doesn't fully cover

1. **Test venue:** Component-rendering tests in `slot-card.integration.test.ts`. Existing dialog tests are there already; update them.

2. **`ha-entity-picker`:** The pattern is already used in this codebase at `ts/slot-card-editor.ts:219`. Reference that for prop names and event names. Specifically: `.hass`, `.value`, `.label`, `.includeDomains`, `@value-changed=${...}`.

3. **Destructive button styling:** The plan provides `ha-button.destructive { --mdc-theme-primary: var(--warning-color, #ffa726); }`. Verify that's the right CSS variable for HA dialogs (look at how existing buttons are styled in this codebase).

4. **Save button disabled state:** The plan shows `.disabled=${this._dialogSaving}`. Existing logic in `_saveConditionChanges` already sets `_dialogSaving`. Verify it stays in sync.

5. **Remove button placement:** The plan puts it in `slot="secondaryAction"` — meaning it sits on the same row as Cancel. The destructive class + warning color should differentiate it visually.

6. **`_clearSlotCondition` and `_setSlotCondition`:** existing methods. Reuse them — don't reimplement.

7. **Empty state's "+ Add a condition" link:** Already calls `_openConditionDialog('add')` from Task 4. Make sure the dialog handles `mode === 'add'` (entity picker is empty, Remove button is hidden).

content.text:
Done.

---

**Status: DONE**

- **Vitest**: 572 tests before → 576 tests after (all passing). Added 4 new tests covering the new dialog (picker pre-fill, Remove vs Add visibility, dialog title, value-changed wiring) and replaced/refactored the old `_deleteConditionEntity` and `_renderConfirmDialog` test blocks.
- **Build**: `npm run build` succeeded (only pre-existing `generate-view.ts` and `slot-card.ts:260,264` warnings about `_config` possibly being undefined — unchanged from main).
- **prek**: All hooks Passed (ruff/mypy/etc skipped — no Python files in scope; "Lint js with yarn" and "Build with yarn" both Passed).
- **Commit**: `a5725f2a feat(card): use ha-entity-picker in unified Manage Condition dialog` — staged only `ts/` and `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/` per the instruction. `yarn.lock` and `package-lock.json` were left unstaged (they were already dirty before this task).
- **Removed symbols**: `_deleteConditionEntity`, `_renderConfirmDialog`, and the `_confirmDialog` state field — all gone. Their associated tests removed too. The `nothing` import is still used (for the conditional Remove button slot). Sanity grep for `_deleteConditionEntity\|datalist\|condition-entity-list\|_renderConfirmDialog\|_confirmDialog` in `ts/slot-card.ts` and `ts/slot-card.styles.ts` returns empty.

**Design decisions made beyond the plan:**

1. **Destructive button color**: Followed the plan literally (`var(--warning-color, #ffa726)`). Note that the previous implementation used `var(--error-color)` for the same `.destructive` class. The new amber is less alarming, which fits "delete one entity" better than red. If someone wants the harder red back, swap `--warning-color` for `--error-color`. No other usages of `.destructive` remain in the codebase, so the swap is contained.

2. **Removed CSS classes**: While replacing `.dialog-content` styling I also removed several now-unused dialog classes (`.dialog-section`, `.dialog-section-header`, `.dialog-section-description`, `.dialog-checkbox-row`, `.dialog-number-input`, `.dialog-clear-button`, `.confirm-dialog-content`, `.entity-select`). Verified via grep across `ts/` that nothing else references them. This is the natural cleanup the plan implies; flagging it explicitly in case any of those were intended for a later task.

3. **`_saveConditionChanges` validation kept**: Even though `ha-entity-picker` normally only emits valid IDs, I left the existing "entity not in `hass.states`" guard in place since the dialog still calls the same shared method. The existing tests around it continue to pass.

4. **`_removeCondition` error handling**: On failure I leave the dialog open (so the user sees the action error banner above the card), matching the existing `_saveConditionChanges` pattern. The test for the failure path asserts this.

5. **Manage-mode "no entity" edge**: If `_openConditionDialog('manage')` is somehow invoked when `condition_entity` is undefined (currently impossible from the UI, since the Manage link only renders when an entity exists), the picker opens empty and the Remove button still shows. Left as-is — costs nothing and is robust if the rendering precondition ever loosens.

**Concerns**: None blocking. The `--warning-color` choice is the only judgment call; everything else was prescribed by the plan.

# Your task — Task 6: Activity event row (replaces fake activity log)

A single click-through row at the bottom of the card showing `🕒 Last used : Front Door · 3h ago →`. Click → opens HA more-info on the event entity. No collapsible chrome, no fake activity log.

The previous header had a `last-used` pill — that's already gone (Task 1 stripped header pills). This task adds the new row at the bottom of `_renderFromData`.

**Read the plan's Task 6 section completely** before starting. The plan provides:
- Failing test code
- The new `_renderEventRow` method body
- New CSS for `.event-row`, `.event-icon`, `.event-name`, `.event-meta`, `.event-arrow`
- Direction to wire `${this._renderEventRow()}` after the Conditions/Locks sections in `_renderFromData`

## Notes the plan doesn't fully cover

1. **Test venue:** `slot-card.integration.test.ts` (per the established Phase 2 pattern).

2. **`_navigateToEventHistory` was removed in Task 1** (since the header no longer has the pill that called it). Re-add it now — it's the click handler for the event row. The implementation existed before; recreate it:
   ```typescript
   private _navigateToEventHistory(): void {
       const eventEntityId = this._data?.event_entity_id;
       if (!eventEntityId) return;
       const event = new CustomEvent('hass-more-info', {
           bubbles: true,
           composed: true,
           detail: { entityId: eventEntityId },
       });
       this.dispatchEvent(event);
   }
   ```

3. **Icon imports:** Add `mdiClockOutline` and `mdiChevronRight` to the imports.

4. **`_renderEventRow` returns `nothing` when no event entity** — make sure the row simply doesn't render rather than rendering an empty div.

5. **`Never used` empty state:** When `event_entity_id` exists but `last_used` is null (slot configured but PIN never used), the row should render `🕒 Last used   Never used   →`. Click still opens more-info on the event entity.

6. **`ha-relative-time`:** Use the HA component (already used elsewhere in this file). Pattern: `<ha-relative-time .hass=${this._hass} .datetime=${lastUsed}></ha-relative-time>`.

7. **Border treatment:** The plan's CSS doesn't specify a border between the event row and the section above. Check the mockup at `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html` — the event row sits naturally at the bottom of the card with hover background. No top border needed if the gap from `.content` flexbox provides enough separation.

8. **`show_lock_count` / `show_lock_status`:** the old header had a lock pill controlled by `show_lock_count`. That pill is gone. Don't worry about reconnecting `show_lock_count` to anything in Task 6 — it can become a no-op for now (Task 7 will sweep dead config options).

# Verification

```bash
npm test
npm run build
prek run --files ts/slot-card.ts ts/slot-card.styles.ts ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js
```

# Commit


### Task 7 — Audit & dead style cleanup

### Task 8 — `config_entry_title` in payload
- **Already present.** `custom_components/lock_code_manager/websocket.py:885` already sets `ATTR_CONFIG_ENTRY_TITLE: config_entry.title` in the `_serialize_slot_card_data` result dict, and `ts/types.ts:285` already has `config_entry_title: string` on `SlotCardData`.

The plan's expectation matches reality: the option is dead-code-pathed in the new render but kept for config compatibility, and there are still extensive tests covering its editor behavior so the toggle continues to function as a stored config value. cd ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign && gh pr create --base main --head phase2/slot-card-redesign --title "Slot card redesign — first-principles layout" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Proposed change

Re-architects the LCM slot card frontend per the design captured in the working mockup, replacing data-model-shaped sections (Primary Controls, Status, Conditions, Lock Status) with a journey-shaped layout: identity header → hero row → collapsible Locks → Conditions (HA entity row + LCM overlay + helpers + Manage link) → click-through Activity row.

Changes per area:

- **Header**: small `SLOT N · {config_entry_title}` kicker top-left, colored state chip top-right (`Active` / `Blocked by conditions` / `Disabled by user`), name as a 22px primary title spanning underneath. Pencil button replaces the inline name-edit affordance.
- **Hero row**: PIN (with reveal eye when applicable) + Enable switch always visible at the top of content. Replaces the labeled `Primary Controls` section.
- **Status section**: gone. State lives in the header chip.
- **Conditions**: condition entity now renders as a native HA entity row (via `loadCardHelpers().createRowElement()`) with an LCM overlay strip beneath it (`✓ Allowing access` / `✗ Blocking access` plus context like calendar event / schedule next event). Helpers render as plain HA entity rows under a `Helpers` sub-label. A single `⚙ Manage condition` (or `+ Add a condition`) affordance handles config.
- **Manage Condition dialog**: rebuilt around `ha-entity-picker` (replacing the custom datalist). Single dialog handles add/replace/remove with a destructive `Remove condition` button on the left.
- **Activity**: collapsible replaced with a single click-through `🕒 Last used: <lock> · <relative time> →` row at the bottom of the card. Click opens HA more-info on the event entity (where the actual history lives).
- **Locks**: kept custom (LCM-specific sync state, code value, last-synced timestamp).
- **Backend**: `config_entry_title` was already in the subscribe payload; added regression assertion.

Composition principles:
- HA components first: `ha-entity-picker`, `ha-switch`, `ha-relative-time`, `ha-icon-button`, `ha-svg-icon`, `ha-card`, `ha-dialog`, plus entity rows via `loadCardHelpers`.
- Compose only where LCM-specific framing demands it: state chip, hero row layout, LCM overlay strip on the condition entity, lock rows, event row.

Each commit is independently reviewable and bisectable:

1. `feat(card): redesign header with slot kicker, state chip, and primary name`
2. `feat(card): replace Primary Controls with hero row (PIN + Enable)`
3. `refactor(card): remove Status section (folded into header chip)`
4. `feat(card): refactor Conditions to use HA entity row + LCM overlay`
5. `feat(card): use ha-entity-picker in unified Manage Condition dialog`
6. `feat(card): add click-through event row for last_used`
7. `refactor(card): cleanup dead styles + assert config_entry_title in payload`

## Type of change

- [x] New feature (which adds functionality)

## Additional information

- This PR is related to issue: (none)

### Test plan

- [x] `npm test` — 582 vitest tests passed across 15 files
- [x] `uv run pytest tests/ --timeout=30 -q` — 810 passed
- [x] `prek run --all-files` — all hooks pass
- [x] `npm run build` — bundle regenerated and committed

### Manual verification (recommended before merge)

1. Set up a dev HA instance with an LCM entry titled \"House Locks\" containing a slot named \"Alice\" with a PIN, two locks, and a calendar condition entity (e.g. `calendar.vacation`).
2. Add the card to a Lovelace dashboard (`type: custom:lcm-slot`, `config_entry_title: House Locks`, `slot: 1`).
3. **Active state** (calendar event currently active): green chip \"Active\", green overlay \"✓ Allowing access — <event> · ends in N days\".
4. **Inactive state** (no active event): orange chip \"Blocked by conditions\", orange overlay \"✗ Blocking access — Next: …\".
5. **Disabled state** (toggle off): grey chip \"Disabled by user\", switch off in hero.
6. **Manage Condition dialog**: click `⚙ Manage condition`. Confirm `ha-entity-picker` is pre-filled, restricts to calendar/schedule/binary_sensor/switch/input_boolean. Save with a different entity → card updates. Remove → empty state with `+ Add a condition`. Cancel → no change.
7. **Event row**: click `🕒 Last used: …`. Confirm HA more-info dialog opens for the event entity.

Compare against the working mockup at `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html` (not committed).

### Notes

- `show_lock_count` editor option is preserved for backward compat but is now a no-op for the redesigned layout (commented in `slot-card-editor.ts`).
- The working plan and mockup at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-slot-card-redesign.md` and `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html` are throwaway artifacts and intentionally not staged in any commit. They will be deleted after this PR merges.

EOF

content.text:
**Phase 2 PR opened: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/pull/1116**

To your earlier point: you're right that I shouldn't have proposed cutting reviews to save time. The dual-review pattern caught the migration ordering bug (Copilot) and the multi-entry repair-id collision (code reviewer) in Phase 1, and the code-quality reviewer for Tasks 7+8 confirmed the small-surface deletions were genuinely safe rather than guessing. The cost is real but proportional to the bugs it prevents.

Summary:

- **7 commits**, one per task (Tasks 7+8 bundled since both were small)
- **All checks green** in worktree: 582 vitest, 810 pytest, prek clean across all files
- **Worktree** stays at `~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign`
- **Branch**: `phase2/slot-card-redesign`

Things to do before merging:

1. **Manual visual verification on a real HA instance** — the test suite covers all the structural pieces but can't verify the rendered card looks right. Steps in the PR body. Compare against `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html`.
2. **Watch for Copilot review comments** — Phase 1 caught one real ordering bug there.

When this lands, we can clean up the working artifacts:

- `docs/mockups/first-principles-v2.html`
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-remove-number-of-uses.md`
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-slot-card-redesign.md`

Want me to wait for CI on PR #1116 before considering this turn done, or are we good?

input.description: Fix 5 Copilot review comments on PR #1116
input.prompt:
You are fixing 5 Copilot code review comments on PR #1116 for the `lock_code_manager` Home Assistant custom integration. **Working directory:** `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign` **Branch:** `phase2/slot-card-redesign` (HEAD is `ca3378cc`) **Toolchain:** `npm test`, `npm run build`, `prek run --files `. Vitest baseline 582 tests pass. # The 5 issues ## 1. Event row not keyboard accessible (`ts/slot-card.ts:329`) The `
` is used as an interactive control but has no `role`, `tabindex`, or key handlers. Keyboard and screen-reader users can't open more-info. **Fix:** Add `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, `aria-label="View activity history"` (or similar), and a `@keydown` handler that calls `_navigateToEventHistory` on Enter or Space. ```typescript return html`
this._navigateToEventHistory()} @keydown=${(e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); this._navigateToEventHistory(); } }} > ...
`; ``` ## 2. `Manage condition` / `+ Add a condition` spans not focusable (`ts/slot-card.ts:533`) Same a11y issue as #1. The two `` and `` elements are clickable but not focusable. **Fix:** Add `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and a `@keydown` handler to both. Same pattern as #1. Add an `aria-label` (e.g. "Manage condition" / "Add a condition"). ## 3. `_formatRelative` rounds to "1 day" for ~23h deltas (`ts/slot-card.ts:620`) The docstring says sub-day deltas collapse to "today", but `Math.round(ms / 86400000)` reports "in 1 day" for ~23h timestamps. **Fix:** Check absolute milliseconds first; anything under one day = "today". Then round for the day count: ```typescript private _formatRelative(iso: string): string { const ms = new Date(iso).getTime() - Date.now(); if (Math.abs(ms) < 86400000) return 'today'; const days = Math.round(Math.abs(ms) / 86400000); if (days === 1) return ms > 0 ? 'in 1 day' : '1 day ago'; return ms > 0 ? `in ${days} days` : `${days} days ago`; } ``` Update existing tests in `slot-card.integration.test.ts` if any assert on the old (buggy) behavior. Add a test for the boundary case (~23h delta → "today", ~25h delta → "in 1 day"). ## 4. Race condition: Remove button doesn't disable during `_dialogSaving` (`ts/slot-card.ts:918`) While `_dialogSaving` is true, Save is disabled but Remove is not. Rapid clicks can fire multiple `clear_slot_condition` calls. **Fix:** Two changes: - Add `.disabled=${this._dialogSaving}` to the destructive Remove button in `_renderConditionDialog`. - Add an early return at the top of `_removeCondition`: `if (this._dialogSaving) return;` ## 5. `_navigateToEventHistory` doesn't check entity availability (`ts/slot-card.ts:346`) The comment/spec says the click is a no-op when the entity is unavailable, but the implementation only checks `event_entity_id` is set. **Fix:** Two acceptable options — pick one: - **A**: Strengthen the check: also verify `this._hass?.states[eventEntityId]?.state !== 'unavailable'`. If unavailable, no-op. Maybe also return `nothing` from `_renderEventRow` if the entity is unavailable (so the row simply doesn't render and isn't clickable). - **B**: Update the docstring/comment to match the actual behavior (the implementation always tries to dispatch hass-more-info if the entity_id is set, regardless of state). Pick A — it's the more user-friendly choice. If the entity is unavailable, don't render the row at all (cleaner than rendering a click-through to a more-info dialog that would show nothing useful). # Verification ```bash npm test npm run build prek run --files ts/slot-card.ts ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js ``` # Commit Per project convention `feedback_no_amend_after_pr.md`: don't amend after PR is open. Make a new commit: ```bash git add ts/ custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/ git commit -m "fix(card): address Copilot review on PR #1116 - Add keyboard accessibility (role=button, tabindex, Enter/Space) to event row and Manage/Add condition affordances. - Fix _formatRelative reporting 'in 1 day' for sub-24h deltas. - Disable Remove button during _dialogSaving and add re-entry guard in _removeCondition. - Skip rendering event row when the event entity is unavailable." git push ``` # Reporting back Status: `DONE` / `DONE_WITH_CONCERNS` / `NEEDS_CONTEXT` / `BLOCKED`. Include: - Test counts before/after - Build result - prek result - Commit SHA - Push confirmation - Which option you chose for #5 and why - Any concerns about the keyboard a11y test coverage

content.text:
## Status: DONE

### Summary

All 5 Copilot review comments addressed. Changes pushed to `phase2/slot-card-redesign`.

### Verification

- **Tests**: 582 → 592 passing (10 new tests, 0 failures)
- **Build**: Succeeded; `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` regenerated. Pre-existing TypeScript warnings (unrelated to this change: `_config` possibly undefined in `slot-card.ts:269/273`, `pinActiveEntity` typing in `generate-view.ts:318`) remain.
- **prek**: Passed (lint + build hooks both green)
- **Commit SHA**: `b041bea028c64cd071d953f2c668d1020d49640f`
- **Push**: confirmed (`ca3378cc..b041bea0`)

### Choice for issue #5

Picked **Option A** as instructed: `_renderEventRow` returns `nothing` when `this._hass?.states[eventEntityId]?.state === 'unavailable'`, and `_navigateToEventHistory` got the same guard for defense in depth. Cleaner UX — no dead row that opens an empty more-info dialog.

### Files changed

- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.ts` — fixes #1–#5 in source
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.styles.ts` — added `:focus-visible` outlines for `.event-row`, `.manage-link`, `.add-link`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts` — 10 new tests:
  - `_formatRelative`: 23h boundary → "today", 25h → "in 1 day"
  - event row: returns `nothing` when entity unavailable; `_navigateToEventHistory` no-op when unavailable; role/tabindex/aria-label assertions; Enter/Space dispatch + Tab no-op
  - conditions body: a11y attrs on add-link & manage-link; Enter/Space open dialog, Tab does not
  - `_removeCondition`: re-entry guard test (skips callWS when `_dialogSaving` already true)
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` — regenerated bundle

### Concerns about keyboard a11y test coverage

Two minor caveats worth flagging:

1. **Tests exercise the handler in isolation, not real keyboard dispatch.** They invoke the `@keydown` lambda directly with a synthetic `{key, preventDefault}` object rather than dispatching a real `KeyboardEvent` against the rendered DOM. That verifies the routing logic but not the wiring (`tabindex`/`role` are asserted via string match on the template). For full confidence, an end-to-end test that focuses the element and dispatches a real KeyboardEvent would be stronger — but this matches the existing test style in the file and avoids fighting jsdom focus quirks.

2. **The `:focus-visible` styling isn't covered by tests** since it's CSS-only. Visual verification on a live HA instance would catch any contrast/offset issues before merge.

## Status: DONE

### Summary

All 5 Copilot review comments addressed. Changes pushed to `phase2/slot-card-redesign`.

### Verification

- **Tests**: 582 → 592 passing (10 new tests, 0 failures)
- **Build**: Succeeded; `custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` regenerated. Pre-existing TypeScript warnings (unrelated to this change: `_config` possibly undefined in `slot-card.ts:269/273`, `pinActiveEntity` typing in `generate-view.ts:318`) remain.
- **prek**: Passed (lint + build hooks both green)
- **Commit SHA**: `b041bea028c64cd071d953f2c668d1020d49640f`
- **Push**: confirmed (`ca3378cc..b041bea0`)

### Choice for issue #5

Picked **Option A** as instructed: `_renderEventRow` returns `nothing` when `this._hass?.states[eventEntityId]?.state === 'unavailable'`, and `_navigateToEventHistory` got the same guard for defense in depth. Cleaner UX — no dead row that opens an empty more-info dialog.

### Files changed

- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.ts` — fixes #1–#5 in source
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.styles.ts` — added `:focus-visible` outlines for `.event-row`, `.manage-link`, `.add-link`
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/ts/slot-card.integration.test.ts` — 10 new tests:
  - `_formatRelative`: 23h boundary → "today", 25h → "in 1 day"
  - event row: returns `nothing` when entity unavailable; `_navigateToEventHistory` no-op when unavailable; role/tabindex/aria-label assertions; Enter/Space dispatch + Tab no-op
  - conditions body: a11y attrs on add-link & manage-link; Enter/Space open dialog, Tab does not
  - `_removeCondition`: re-entry guard test (skips callWS when `_dialogSaving` already true)
- `/Users/raman/.config/superpowers/worktrees/lock_code_manager/phase2-slot-card-redesign/custom_components/lock_code_manager/www/generated/lock-code-manager.js` — regenerated bundle

### Concerns about keyboard a11y test coverage

Two minor caveats worth flagging:

1. **Tests exercise the handler in isolation, not real keyboard dispatch.** They invoke the `@keydown` lambda directly with a synthetic `{key, preventDefault}` object rather than dispatching a real `KeyboardEvent` against the rendered DOM. That verifies the routing logic but not the wiring (`tabindex`/`role` are asserted via string match on the template). For full confidence, an end-to-end test that focuses the element and dispatches a real KeyboardEvent would be stronger — but this matches the existing test style in the file and avoids fighting jsdom focus quirks.

2. **The `:focus-visible` styling isn't covered by tests** since it's CSS-only. Visual verification on a live HA instance would catch any contrast/offset issues before merge.
